target.name: KoBold Metals

methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $6.0B.

private.name: Electric Hydrogen

private.name: Amogy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $700M

data.narrative:
KoBold Metals is a Series C climate and energy technology company founded by Kurt House, Josh Goldman, and Jeff Jurinak. The target has raised $400M+ to date and carries a current post-money valuation of $2.0B+. The comparable universe comprises nine private companies and one public company (Linamar, LNZA) at the same or adjacent stage within climate and energy infrastructure.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchored to the $2.0B Series C post-money valuation, applies a 1.7x multiplier typical of the Series C-to-next-stage step-up, yielding $3.4B. Peer-median analysis across nine same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—including Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B, Series C), Amogy ($700M, Series C), and Redwood Materials ($6.0B, Series D)—produces a median valuation of $500M, with a range spanning $120M to $6.0B. The dispersion reflects heterogeneity in maturity and capital intensity within the climate tech cohort.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $425M (low) to $3.9B (high), with a median of $1.9B. The current $2.0B valuation falls near the mid-point, though significant uncertainty persists. Key drivers of this dispersion include stage heterogeneity within the peer set, varying capital requirements across subsectors, and wide variance in exit multiples for frontier energy technologies. The peer-median method may underweight KoBold's Series C stage relative to the stage-progression approach.
